exampleSentencesTranslated: The older sister buys two loaves of bread.
The older sister drinks a glass of iced tea.
notes:
chị = older sister. It’s used for your real older sister, but also as a pronoun for a woman slightly older than you.

In families, it contrasts with em (younger sibling). To be clear, people often say chị gái (older sister). When counting, use người chị if you need a classifier.

As a pronoun, chị can mean “I” (if she’s older than the listener) or “you” (if she’s older than you). Think of it as age-based, not just family-based.

translation.en: I met my sister at school.

sentenceBreakdowns: chị (older sister) + mua (buy) + hai (two) + cái (classifier) + bánh mì (bread)
chị (older sister) + uống (drink) + một (one) + ly (glass) + trà đá (iced tea)
chị (older sister) + xem (watch) + phim hoạt hình (cartoons)
notes_md:
**chị** = older sister. It’s used for your real older sister, but also as a pronoun for a woman slightly older than you.

In families, it contrasts with **em** (younger sibling). To be clear, people often say **chị gái** (older sister). When counting, use **người chị** if you need a classifier.

As a pronoun, **chị** can mean “I” (if she’s older than the listener) or “you” (if she’s older than you). Think of it as age-based, not just family-based.
exampleSentences: chị mua hai cái bánh mì.
chị uống một ly trà đá.

definitions.en: An informal term used to refer to an older sister or a female friend.
definitions.es: Un término informal utilizado para referirse a una hermana mayor o a una amiga.
